page: Amazon Web Services g6f.2xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.4 GHz, configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For graphics and parallel computing acceleration, it includes a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU with a 0.25 allocation and 6 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage consists of a 200 GB NVMe SSD, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core testing placing in the top 25 percent. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and Passmark CPU metrics fall within the average tier. Memory performance shows a distinct tradeoff: while cached small-block read bandwidth is average, large-block uncached read and write memory bandwidths are weak, falling into the bottom 25 percent. In LLM inference tests, the instance demonstrates top-tier performance in the top 10 percent for prompt processing with a medium 7B model, while text generation for the same model remains average.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the fractional GPU allocation allows workloads to access hardware-accelerated graphics and machine learning capabilities without the expense of a full GPU. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage provides low-latency data access. This resource balance makes the instance suitable for entry-level machine learning inference, lightweight graphics rendering, media transcoding, and general-purpose workloads requiring modest GPU acceleration alongside balanced compute and memory resources.
description: Amazon Web Services g6f.2xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It integrates a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU with 6 GB of VRAM and a 200 GB local NVMe SSD. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier prompt processing speeds for medium 7B LLM models, though large-block uncached memory bandwidth is weak. With 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for workloads requiring modest GPU acceleration, such as lightweight machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and media transcoding, without the cost of a dedicated full GPU.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6f.2xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, a 200 GB NVMe SSD, and a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU with 6 GB VRAM for accelerated workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6f.2xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, a 200 GB NVMe SSD, and a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU.
tagline: A balanced virtual machine featuring fractional GPU acceleration, dedicated AMD EPYC processing, and local NVMe storage for entry-level accelerated workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor at 3.4 GHz
Includes a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU with 6 GB of VRAM
Equipped with 32 GB DDR4 RAM and 200 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and top-tier 7B LLM prompt processing performance
Suited for lightweight machine learning inference and graphics rendering
